mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers real discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the easing trend is persistent rather than a one-month noise move.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in easing episodes where FEDFUNDS declines over 3 consecutive monthly observations, or if the sign turns negative in more than half of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above a recession-warning threshold signals weakening household income and profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the crossing occurs.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of UNRATE-crossing events, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not below unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 and RSAFS both accelerate together, the signal implies broad demand strength and better near-term earnings visibility, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint high-growth episodes in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO without a matching inflation surge signals improving real activity and stable margins, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises month-over-month while CPIAUCSL monthly inflation remains below 0.3%
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO upticks in low-inflation months, or if positive returns occur equally often when INDPRO rises and CPI accelerates.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies labor-market deterioration that compresses earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 55% of the time, or if either series alone explains the sign as well as the pair.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS regime above neutral suppresses equity multiples and raises recession odds, so S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days should be negative while policy remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive FEDFUNDS episodes produce positive 60-day SPX returns in more than half of cases, or if the average return is not below zero by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound, measured by HOUST rising from a depressed base, signals future demand spillovers into consumption and cyclical equities, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-rebound episodes fail to outperform baseline on 20-day SPX returns, or if rising HOUST after a decline is followed by negative returns in most occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while unemployment stays flat, the economy is expanding on a real production basis without labor stress, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is unchanged or lower over 3 months while INDPRO trends higher
failure_condition: Reject if soft-landing episodes do not yield positive 60-day SPX returns more often than unconditional periods, or if the combination of rising INDPRO and stable UNRATE adds no predictive value.
